text: DEIMoS functionality includes feature detection, feature alignment, collision cross section calibration, isotope detection, and MS/MS spectral deconvolution.
source_excerpt:
Functionality includes feature detection, feature alignment, collision cross section (CCS) calibration, 
isotope detection, and MS/MS spectral deconvolution

text: DEIMoS algorithm implementations increase alignment and feature matching confidence among datasets through simultaneous use of all dimensions.
source_excerpt: (ii) increase alignment/feature matching confidence among datasets

text: Alignment is the process by which feature coordinates across samples are adjusted to account for instrument variation such that matching features are aligned.
source_excerpt: Alignment is the process by which feature coordinates across samples are adjusted to account for instrument variation (drift, calibration, etc.) such that matching features are aligned to adjust for small differences in coordinates.

text: DEIMoS implements reference-based alignment by constructing a model for each dimension of a sample by matching detected features against a reference sample.
source_excerpt: To perform alignment, we can construct a model for each dimension of a sample by putatively matching detected features against an in-study reference sample, minimizing the residual, and subsequently applying the fit transform.

text: Cross-sample alignment involves matching corresponding features across data sets within a user defined tolerance.
source_excerpt: We can also match corresponding features across data sets within a user defined tolerance.
